name: Trivikrama Pandita
dates_notes: Direct disciple of Madhva; near-contemporary with Madhva (born within Madhva's lifetime, active until shortly after Madhva's mahā-samādhi). Father of Nārāyaṇa-Paṇḍitācārya, the author of the Sumadhva-Vijaya.

lineage_polemical.note: Trivikrama is the direct interlocutor whose Vāyu-Stuti and Tattva-Pradīpa establish the form in which the first generation of Madhva-disciples received the master's teaching.

core_thesis:
Trivikrama Paṇḍitācārya is the principal direct disciple of Madhva and the author of the *Tattva-Pradīpa* — the earliest sub-commentary on Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*. The doctrinal architecture is Madhva's own, transmitted without revision: *pañca-bheda* (Brahman/jīva, jīva/jīva, Brahman/jaḍa, jīva/jaḍa, jaḍa/jaḍa) as the structure of reality (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 2.1, on *bheda-prakaraṇa*); *svatantra*-and-*paratantra* as the load-bearing distinction (Viṣṇu the only *svatantra*; everything else *paratantra*) (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 1.1.2); the *bimba-pratibimba* (original-and-image) relation between Viṣṇu and *jīva* with strict asymmetry (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 2.3.43, on the *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa*); the doctrine of the *sākṣi* (witness-consciousness) as autonomous *pramāṇa* (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad relevant *adhikaraṇas*); the realist construal of the world as Viṣṇu's real *sṛṣṭi* against any reduction to appearance.

The work is two-fold. First, the *Tattva-Pradīpa* ("lamp on the truth") on the *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* — the school's earliest sub-commentary on Madhva's *sūtra*-commentary, predating Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Prakāśikā* by approximately one generation. The work fixes the first-generation reading of Madhva's compressed glosses in a form that is preserved in the lineage's manuscript transmission and that Jayatīrtha presupposes (Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Prakāśikā* refines and supersedes but does not replace; the *Tattva-Pradīpa* remains in the school's manuscript canon). Second, the *Vāyu-Stuti* — a 41-verse hymn to Vāyu (in Madhva's theology, Viṣṇu's principal *bhakta* and the lineage-presider as Hanumān, Bhīma, and Madhva himself) — that fixes the doctrine of the *Tāratamya* (the hierarchical ordering of beings under Viṣṇu, with Vāyu as next in rank) in liturgical form for the school's daily *japa*. The hymn is transmitted by every Mādhva *maṭha* and is the principal liturgical text identifying Madhva as the third *avatāra* of Vāyu (the *Hari-Vāyu-Stuti* tradition fixed at [*Vāyu-Stuti* vv](cite://trivikrama-pandita/vayu-stuti/vv). 39–41). Together the two works fix the first-generation Mādhva reception of Madhva's teaching in the form the lineage will transmit.

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Pradipa on Brahma-Sutra-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Madhva's Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya, preserved in the Mādhva manuscript canon and cited by Jayatīrtha and later sub-commentators.

ascription_evidence.description: Manuscript colophons across the Mādhva-paramparā preservation tradition.

engaged_works.summary: The *Tattva-Pradīpa* ("lamp on the truth") is Trivikrama Paṇḍitācārya's sub-commentary on Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* — the earliest Mādhva sub-commentary on the *sūtra*-text. The method is *adhikaraṇa*-by-*adhikaraṇa* exposition with the school's settled position fixed at each contested point, in a form that predates Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Prakāśikā* by approximately one generation. Three loci do the load-bearing work. (i) The opening *jijñāsādhikaraṇa* (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad [BS 1.1.1](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.1)) reads the inquiry as inquiry into a real Viṣṇu, against Śaṅkara's *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* construal that takes the inquiry as into a Brahman identified with the *jīva*-self by superimposition; the *upodghāta* fixes the *prameya*-economy of the school. (ii) The *ānanda-mayādhikaraṇa* (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad [BS 1.1.12–19](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.12-19)) is settled in favour of the realist reading of *ānandamaya* as Viṣṇu, against Śaṅkara's *jīva*-reading; the argument follows Madhva's *Bhāṣya* and clarifies the school's reading of the *taittirīya-upaniṣad* passage on which the *adhikaraṇa* turns. (iii) The *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa* (*Tattva-Pradīpa* ad [BS 2.3.43](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.3.43)), with its *ghaṭākāśa* (pot-and-space) analogy, is read as articulating asymmetric dependence (the *jīva* as part of Viṣṇu in the dependence-sense, *bimba-pratibimba* with strict asymmetry) rather than identity-with-modification. The *Tattva-Pradīpa* is the form in which the first-generation Mādhva community received Madhva's *sūtra*-commentary; Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Prakāśikā* refines and supersedes for higher scholastic study, but the *Tattva-Pradīpa* remains in the school's manuscript canon as the earliest direct-disciple reading of the *sūtras*.

engaged_works.title: Vayu-Stuti
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored hymn; recited daily in the Mādhva-paramparā as part of the standard liturgical canon; the text is preserved in every Mādhva maṭha.

ascription_evidence.description: Continuous Mādhva-paramparā transmission as part of daily liturgy.

engaged_works.summary: A 41-verse Sanskrit hymn to Vāyu — in Madhva's theology, Viṣṇu's principal *bhakta* and the lineage-presider, identified by the school as having taken three *avatāras*: Hanumān (in the *Rāmāyaṇa*), Bhīma (in the *Mahābhārata*), and Madhva himself. The hymn opens with a *maṅgala*-frame addressed to Hari and Vāyu ("Hari-Vāyu-Stuti" — the title under which it circulates) and proceeds through 41 *sragdharā*-and-related-metre verses laying out the doctrine of the *Tāratamya* (the hierarchical ordering of beings under Viṣṇu, with Vāyu as next in rank) and the threefold-*avatāra* identification ([*Vāyu-Stuti* vv](cite://trivikrama-pandita/vayu-stuti/vv). 39–41 are the locus classicus of the school's identification of Madhva as the third *avatāra* of Vāyu). The hymn is the principal Mādhva liturgical text addressed to the lineage-presider and is recited daily in every Mādhva *maṭha*; it functions doctrinally as the lineage's affirmation of the *Tāratamya* and devotionally as the standard *japa*-text. The *Vāyu-Stuti* is the most widely transmitted of Trivikrama's works and is the principal vehicle through which his name remains alive in the school's daily practice.
